usageGuidance: Append when the work required to provide a service is substantially greater than typically required. Must be supported by documentation.

examples.correct: Complex wound closure on a morbidly obese patient requiring significantly more time and effort
examples.incorrect: Routine procedure that took slightly longer than expected

commonErrors: Using without supporting documentation
Applying to E/M services
Using for minor additional work

description: Unrelated Evaluation and Management Service by the Same Physician During a Postoperative Period
usageGuidance: Use when an E/M service is performed during a postoperative period for a reason unrelated to the original procedure.

examples.correct: Patient seen for new onset chest pain during 90-day global period for knee surgery
examples.incorrect: Follow-up visit for the surgical site during the global period

commonErrors: Using for related conditions
Failing to link to a different diagnosis

description: Significant, Separately Identifiable Evaluation and Management Service by the Same Physician on the Same Day of the Procedure
usageGuidance: Append to E/M code when a significant, separately identifiable E/M service is performed on the same day as a procedure.

examples.correct: Patient presents with URI symptoms and a suspicious mole is biopsied during the same visit
examples.incorrect: Decision to perform a minor procedure during the same visit without separate E/M documentation

commonErrors: Using without a separate diagnosis or clinical indication
Not documenting the separate E/M service
Using with 0-day global procedures without justification

usageGuidance: Use when only the professional component (interpretation and report) of a procedure is performed. The technical component is billed separately.

examples.correct: Radiologist interprets a chest X-ray performed at an independent facility
examples.incorrect: Physician performs and interprets an X-ray in their own office (bill global)

commonErrors: Using when the physician owns the equipment and performs both components
Applying to services without a TC/26 split

usageGuidance: Use when services are mandated by a governmental body or third-party payer.

examples.correct: Independent medical examination required by workers' compensation
examples.incorrect: Routine exam requested by the patient's employer for fitness-for-duty

commonErrors: Using for non-mandated services
Confusing with modifier 33

usageGuidance: Use when a service is specifically identified as preventive under applicable laws. Typically waives cost-sharing requirements.

examples.correct: Screening colonoscopy for patient over 45 with no symptoms
examples.incorrect: Diagnostic colonoscopy performed due to rectal bleeding

commonErrors: Using for diagnostic procedures
Applying to services not on the preventive services list

usageGuidance: Use when the surgeon provides regional or general anesthesia in addition to performing the surgical procedure.

examples.correct: Surgeon administers regional block and performs carpal tunnel release in the office
examples.incorrect: Surgeon administers local anesthesia as part of a routine procedure

commonErrors: Using for local anesthesia (which is included in the procedure)
Using when a separate anesthesiologist is present

usageGuidance: Use when a procedure is performed bilaterally during the same operative session. Typically reimbursed at 150% of the unilateral rate.

examples.correct: Bilateral inguinal hernia repair performed in a single session
examples.incorrect: Procedure inherently bilateral (check code descriptor)

commonErrors: Using with codes that already describe bilateral procedures
Using instead of RT/LT modifiers when payer requires laterality

usageGuidance: Use when multiple procedures are performed at the same session by the same provider. Secondary procedures are typically reduced.

examples.correct: Excision of two separate lesions during the same operative session
examples.incorrect: Add-on codes (marked with + in CPT) which are exempt from modifier 51

commonErrors: Applying to add-on codes
Not listing the highest-value procedure first
Using with modifier 51-exempt codes

usageGuidance: Use when a service or procedure is partially reduced or eliminated at the discretion of the physician.

examples.correct: Planned bilateral procedure where only one side was completed due to patient tolerance
examples.incorrect: Procedure completed as planned but took less time than average

commonErrors: Using because a procedure was faster than expected
Confusing with modifier 53 (discontinued procedure)

usageGuidance: Use when a procedure is discontinued due to risk to the patient's well-being after anesthesia administration or procedure initiation.

examples.correct: Colonoscopy discontinued due to patient's adverse reaction to sedation
examples.incorrect: Procedure cancelled before the patient was prepped

commonErrors: Using when procedure was never started
Using for elective cancellations
Confusing with modifier 73/74 for ASC settings

usageGuidance: Use when one physician performs the surgical procedure and another provides preoperative and/or postoperative care.

examples.correct: Surgeon performs hip replacement; patient transfers postoperative care to a different physician
examples.incorrect: Surgeon provides complete surgical care including follow-up

commonErrors: Not having a transfer of care agreement documented
Using without modifiers 55/56 on the corresponding claims

usageGuidance: Use when one physician provides postoperative care only, with the surgery performed by another physician.

examples.correct: Physician assumes postoperative management after patient relocates during the global period
examples.incorrect: Follow-up visit by the operating surgeon

commonErrors: Not documenting transfer of care
Billing before transfer date

usageGuidance: Use when one physician performs the preoperative care and evaluation and another physician performs the surgery.

examples.correct: Internist performs preoperative clearance for cardiac surgery
examples.incorrect: Surgeon's own preoperative evaluation

commonErrors: Using for routine preoperative clearance billed as E/M
Not coordinating with the surgical modifier 54

usageGuidance: Use when an E/M service results in the initial decision to perform surgery (major procedure with 90-day global period).

examples.correct: ER visit where physician evaluates and decides to perform an appendectomy
examples.incorrect: E/M visit where a minor procedure with 0-day global is decided upon (use modifier 25)

commonErrors: Using with 0-day or 10-day global procedures (use 25 instead)
Not documenting the decision-making process

description: Staged or Related Procedure During the Postoperative Period
usageGuidance: Use for a procedure during a postop period that was planned at the time of the original surgery, or is more extensive, or for therapy following a diagnostic surgical procedure.

examples.correct: Planned second-stage reconstruction during the postoperative period of the first stage
examples.incorrect: Unrelated procedure performed during a global period (use modifier 79)

commonErrors: Using for unrelated procedures (use 79)
Using for treatment of complications (use 78)

usageGuidance: Use to indicate that a procedure or service was distinct or independent from other services performed on the same day. This is a modifier of last resort.

examples.correct: Different operative site, separate incision, separate encounter, or different organ system
examples.incorrect: Attempting to unbundle services that are integral to a primary procedure

commonErrors: Overuse to bypass CCI edits without clinical justification
Not using more specific X modifiers (XE, XP, XS, XU) when appropriate
Using to unbundle inherently bundled services

usageGuidance: Use when two surgeons work together as primary surgeons performing distinct portions of a single procedure.

examples.correct: Anterior/posterior spinal fusion where one surgeon performs the anterior approach and another the posterior
examples.incorrect: Surgeon and assistant surgeon (use modifier 80)

commonErrors: Confusing with co-surgeon situations
Not documenting each surgeon's distinct role

usageGuidance: Use when a surgical procedure requires a team of physicians, each performing a distinct part of the procedure.

examples.correct: Complex multi-organ transplant requiring multiple surgical specialists
examples.incorrect: Simple procedure with an assistant surgeon

commonErrors: Using for procedures not requiring team approach
Not documenting team necessity

usageGuidance: Use when a procedure or service is repeated by the same physician subsequent to the original procedure.

examples.correct: Second chest X-ray on the same day by the same radiologist after chest tube placement
examples.incorrect: Repeat procedure performed by a different physician (use modifier 77)

commonErrors: Confusing with modifier 77 (different physician)
Not documenting medical necessity for the repeat

usageGuidance: Use when a procedure is repeated by a different physician on the same day.

examples.correct: Second interpretation of an ECG by a different cardiologist on the same day
examples.incorrect: Same physician repeating a procedure (use modifier 76)

commonErrors: Using when the same physician repeats the procedure
Not documenting why the repeat was necessary

description: Unplanned Return to the Operating Room for a Related Procedure During the Postoperative Period
usageGuidance: Use for an unplanned return to the operating room for a procedure related to the initial surgery during the postop period.

examples.correct: Return to OR for post-surgical hemorrhage control
examples.incorrect: Planned staged procedure during the postoperative period (use modifier 58)

commonErrors: Using for planned returns (use 58)
Using for unrelated procedures (use 79)

description: Unrelated Procedure During the Postoperative Period
usageGuidance: Use when a procedure is performed during the postoperative period of another procedure and is unrelated to the original surgery.

examples.correct: Appendectomy during the global period of a knee replacement
examples.incorrect: Related procedure or complication of the original surgery

commonErrors: Using for related procedures
Not linking to a different diagnosis

usageGuidance: Use when an assistant surgeon actively assists during a surgical procedure.

examples.correct: General surgeon assisting an orthopedic surgeon during complex spinal surgery
examples.incorrect: Surgical first assistant who is not a physician (use modifier 81 or AS)

commonErrors: Using for non-physician assistants
Billing without documentation of medical necessity for an assistant

usageGuidance: Use when a physician assists for a portion of a procedure, providing minimum assistance.

examples.correct: Physician assists briefly during a specific portion of a lengthy surgery
examples.incorrect: Full assistant surgeon participation throughout the case

commonErrors: Using when full assistant services were provided (use 80)
Not documenting the limited extent of assistance

description: Assistant Surgeon (when qualified resident surgeon not available)
usageGuidance: Use in teaching hospitals when a qualified resident is unavailable and another surgeon acts as assistant.

examples.correct: No qualified resident available for a complex cardiac case at a teaching hospital
examples.incorrect: Teaching hospital with available qualified residents

commonErrors: Using when qualified residents are available
Not documenting resident unavailability

usageGuidance: Use when the same laboratory test is repeated on the same day to obtain subsequent results.

examples.correct: Serial troponin levels drawn 6 hours apart to assess for MI
examples.incorrect: Lab test repeated because of equipment malfunction or specimen issues

commonErrors: Using for reruns due to specimen problems
Using for confirmation testing

usageGuidance: Use when a lab test is performed using a kit or portable instrument at the point-of-care.

examples.correct: CLIA-waived rapid strep test performed in the office
examples.incorrect: Standard lab test performed on the primary laboratory platform

commonErrors: Using for standard laboratory platform tests
Using without a CLIA certificate of waiver

description: Synchronous Telemedicine Service via Telephone or Other Real-Time Interactive Audio-Only Telecommunications System
usageGuidance: Use for real-time audio-only telemedicine encounters. Check payer-specific coverage policies.

examples.correct: Physician conducts a follow-up visit via telephone for medication management
examples.incorrect: Audio-video telemedicine visit (use modifier 95 or POS 02)

commonErrors: Using for audio-video visits
Not verifying payer coverage for audio-only services

description: Synchronous Telemedicine Service via Real-Time Interactive Audio and Video Telecommunications System
usageGuidance: Use for real-time audio-video telemedicine encounters. Append to the same codes used for in-person services.

examples.correct: Video visit for established patient E/M service 99214
examples.incorrect: Telephone-only visit (use modifier 93)

commonErrors: Using for audio-only encounters
Not documenting the telemedicine modality
Using with POS 02 and modifier 95 simultaneously (payer-dependent)

usageGuidance: Use to identify the primary physician when multiple physicians are involved in a patient's care.

examples.correct: Primary physician in a shared care arrangement
examples.incorrect: Solo physician providing all care

commonErrors: Using when only one physician is involved

usageGuidance: Use to identify the principal physician of record for inpatient services.

examples.correct: Admitting physician identified as principal physician of record for DRG payment
examples.incorrect: Consulting physician

commonErrors: Using for consulting physicians
Applying to outpatient services

usageGuidance: Use when services are rendered by a non-participating physician or supplier.

examples.correct: Non-par physician providing emergency services
examples.incorrect: Participating physician billing Medicare

commonErrors: Using when the provider is a participating provider

usageGuidance: Use to indicate that chiropractic manipulation is being performed for acute treatment. Required by Medicare for chiropractic services.

examples.correct: Chiropractic manipulation for acute low back pain
examples.incorrect: Maintenance chiropractic care

commonErrors: Using for maintenance therapy
Omitting when required by Medicare for chiropractic services

description: Waiver of Liability Statement Issued as Required by Payer Policy (ABN on file)
usageGuidance: Use when an ABN (Advance Beneficiary Notice) has been issued to the patient and is on file. Indicates the provider expects denial.

examples.correct: ABN obtained before performing a screening service not covered by Medicare
examples.incorrect: Service expected to be covered without an ABN

commonErrors: Using without a valid signed ABN on file
Confusing with GX or GZ modifiers

description: Notice of Liability Issued, Voluntary Under Payer Policy
usageGuidance: Use to indicate a voluntary ABN was issued for a service that is statutorily excluded from Medicare coverage.

examples.correct: ABN issued for a service that is never covered by Medicare (statutory exclusion)
examples.incorrect: Service that may be covered but requires medical necessity review (use GA)

commonErrors: Confusing with GA (mandatory ABN)
Using for services that are potentially covered

description: Item or Service Statutorily Excluded or Does Not Meet the Definition of Any Medicare Benefit
usageGuidance: Use to indicate a service is not a Medicare benefit. Used for mandatory claims submission to obtain a denial for secondary payer billing.

examples.correct: Submitting a claim for hearing aids (not a Medicare benefit) to obtain denial for secondary insurance
examples.incorrect: Service that is potentially covered by Medicare

commonErrors: Using for services that may be covered
Not using when needed for secondary payer claims

description: Item or Service Expected to Be Denied as Not Reasonable and Necessary
usageGuidance: Use when an ABN was NOT issued for a service expected to be denied. Provider accepts liability for the service.

examples.correct: Service performed without ABN that the provider expects Medicare to deny
examples.incorrect: Service with a signed ABN on file (use GA)

commonErrors: Using when an ABN was obtained (use GA instead)
Not understanding that provider accepts financial responsibility

description: Glucose Monitor Supply for Diabetic Beneficiary Not Treated with Insulin
usageGuidance: Use for glucose monitoring supplies for non-insulin-treated diabetic patients.

examples.correct: Test strips for a Type 2 diabetic managed with oral medications only
examples.incorrect: Test strips for an insulin-dependent diabetic

commonErrors: Using for insulin-treated patients
Not verifying diabetes diagnosis

description: Requirements Specified in the Medical Policy Have Been Met
usageGuidance: Use to indicate that specific coverage criteria in an LCD or NCD have been met. Documentation must support the criteria.

examples.correct: Power wheelchair with documentation meeting all LCD mobility criteria
examples.incorrect: Submitting without documentation supporting the coverage criteria

commonErrors: Using without supporting documentation
Not reviewing specific LCD requirements

usageGuidance: Use to identify procedures performed on the left side of the body.

examples.correct: Left knee arthroscopy
examples.incorrect: Bilateral procedure (use modifier 50)

commonErrors: Using with modifier 50 on the same line
Not using when laterality is required by the code

usageGuidance: Use to identify procedures performed on the right side of the body.

examples.correct: Right carpal tunnel release
examples.incorrect: Bilateral procedure (use modifier 50)

commonErrors: Using with modifier 50 on the same line
Not using when laterality is required

usageGuidance: Use when only the technical component of a procedure is being billed. Covers equipment, supplies, technician, and facility costs.

examples.correct: Hospital billing the technical component of an X-ray interpreted by an independent radiologist
examples.incorrect: Physician billing interpretation only (use modifier 26)

commonErrors: Using with modifier 26 on the same claim line
Billing TC when global service was provided

usageGuidance: Use when a service is distinct because it occurred during a separate encounter on the same day. More specific than modifier 59.

examples.correct: Patient returns for a separate medically necessary service later the same day
examples.incorrect: Services performed during the same encounter

commonErrors: Using for same-encounter services
Not documenting separate encounter times

usageGuidance: Use when a service is distinct because it was performed by a different practitioner. More specific than modifier 59.

examples.correct: Two different physicians each perform a distinct procedure on the same patient same day
examples.incorrect: Same physician performing multiple procedures

commonErrors: Using when the same practitioner performs both services

usageGuidance: Use when a service is distinct because it was performed on a separate organ or structure. More specific than modifier 59.

examples.correct: Lesion removal from the arm and a separate lesion removal from the leg
examples.incorrect: Multiple excisions from the same anatomical structure

commonErrors: Using for same-structure procedures
Not documenting the separate structures

usageGuidance: Use when a service is distinct because it does not overlap the usual components of the main service. More specific than modifier 59.

examples.correct: Drug administration service that does not overlap with the primary chemotherapy service
examples.incorrect: Services that are components of the primary procedure

commonErrors: Using for overlapping services
Not justifying the unusual nature of the service
